ranking_basis.risk_score:
risk_score within Accepted assistant guidance:
- Separate entity references from scalar inputs: State which inputs represent named entities, lists of entities, cohorts, products, or account references so the runtime can bind them consistently. Mark each as entity_reference or non-reference and specify the catalog or lookup source when applicable.
  Why: The source includes multiple catalog-backed references and free-text scopes; these must be distinguished to avoid unsafe guessing.
- Define when missing or ambiguous inputs should stop for clarification: Describe which missing fields should trigger clarification_required instead of defaulting or guessing. Include the exact clarification_hint text for each field and note whether the request can proceed with partial inputs.
  Why: The requirements explicitly say missing critical inputs must be clarified, but the stop conditions need to be encoded per field.
- Preserve the inputs needed at approval boundaries: Call out which inputs must survive into approval review so approvers can see the same bounded request context the runtime used. Include any lineage, scope, and target references that must be retained in the approval payload.
  Why: Approval-gated previews are part of the baseline, so the input contract should preserve the exact request context needed for review and audit.

- Separate entity references from scalar inputs: State which inputs represent named entities, lists of entities, cohorts, products, or account references so the runtime can bind them consistently. Mark each as entity_reference or non-reference and specify the catalog or lookup source when applicable.
  Why: The source includes multiple catalog-backed references and free-text scopes; these must be distinguished to avoid unsafe guessing.
- Define when missing or ambiguous inputs should stop for clarification: Describe which missing fields should trigger clarification_required instead of defaulting or guessing. Include the exact clarification_hint text for each field and note whether the request can proceed with partial inputs.
  Why: The requirements explicitly say missing critical inputs must be clarified, but the stop conditions need to be encoded per field.
- Preserve the inputs needed at approval boundaries: Call out which inputs must survive into approval review so approvers can see the same bounded request context the runtime used. Include any lineage, scope, and target references that must be retained in the approval payload.
  Why: Approval-gated previews are part of the baseline, so the input contract should preserve the exact request context needed for review and audit.

result_display.style: Show generated draft content first, then rationale and evidence.

app_glue.reason: Operator review classified this as consuming-app guidance. The contract remains valid; the app profile owns selection, framing, or clarification behavior without changing generic ANIP invocation.

business_language_rules.interpretation: Operator review classified this as consuming-app guidance. The contract remains valid; the app profile owns selection, framing, or clarification behavior without changing generic ANIP invocation. Do not pass vague target wording as a literal input. The consuming app should select a reviewed bounded target group or ask for clarification before invoking the capability.
business_language_rules.meaning: User asks the app or service to choose a target group from business wording such as top, ranked, selected, recommended, or at-risk records.

intent_rules.agent_action: Select, clarify, or frame the request before invoking the ANIP capability.
